about.heroSubtitle: Affordable European city breaks that fit around your working week — Friday evening out, Sunday back.
about.lead: We help Europeans discover affordable short trips — leave Friday evening, return Sunday, and enjoy a full city break without burning vacation days.

sections.weekendP1: Most of us only have Saturday and Sunday free — but that is enough for a European getaway. euroweekender.com is built around short round trips: fly out after work on Friday, explore Saturday, and be back home Sunday evening.
sections.weekendP2: Use trip types like Fri–Sun and the evening departure filter to find flights that match a real working-week schedule, not just mid-week holidays.
sections.whatP: euroweekender.com aggregates weekend flight deals crawled from Kiwi.com and presents them in a simple search experience. Pick your departure airports, choose one or more travel weekends, filter by trip type, and compare round-trip prices at a glance.
sections.whoP: The site is built for city-break travelers, couples, and friends who want inspiration without spending hours on flight comparison sites. Whether you leave from Prague, Vienna, Barcelona, or any other European hub, you can scan upcoming weekends and book directly with the airline or travel partner.
sections.rankP: Deals are sorted by total trip price for your passenger count. We also use hub scores based on offer volume, price levels, and destination variety to suggest strong departure airports near your location.
sections.affiliateP: When you click through to book a flight, you may be redirected to Kiwi.com or another booking partner. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. Prices and availability are always confirmed on the partner site before you pay.
sections.kiwiP1: We link you to Kiwi.com, a well-established European flight booking platform used by millions of travelers each year. Payment runs on Kiwi's secure checkout with industry-standard encryption, and your ticket is issued through their booking system with customer support if plans change.
sections.kiwiP2: Kiwi.com also offers booking protection and disruption assistance on eligible fares, so you are not left on your own when airlines reschedule or cancel. You always review the final price, baggage rules, and conditions on Kiwi.com before confirming payment.

about.cta: Ready to plan your next break?

howItWorks.heroSubtitle: Four simple steps from Friday evening flight to Sunday return — no vacation days required.
howItWorks.lead: Find a cheap European weekend trip in four steps — leave Friday evening, return Sunday, and skip the vacation days.
howItWorks.builtP1: euroweekender.com focuses on short round trips that fit around work. Catch a Friday evening flight after your last meeting, spend Saturday in a new city, and fly home Sunday night — two nights away, zero days of annual leave.
howItWorks.builtP2: Pick a Fri–Sun trip type and turn on the evening departure filter to surface flights that match that rhythm instead of generic week-long searches on other sites.
howItWorks.step1text: Allow location access or pick departure hubs manually. Search from several cities at once.
howItWorks.step2text: Select one or more travel weekends. Each pill shows the outbound date range for that trip.
howItWorks.step3text: Choose Fri–Sun patterns, evening departures, and passenger count. Results update automatically.
howItWorks.step4text: Sort by price, review outbound and return legs, then book securely on Kiwi.com.
howItWorks.kiwiP: Kiwi.com is one of Europe's largest flight search and booking platforms. When you click through from euroweekender.com, you pay on Kiwi's own secure checkout (not on our site). That means encrypted card handling, a proper booking record, and access to Kiwi's help team if something goes wrong with your trip.
howItWorks.tip1: Try multiple nearby airports — smaller hubs sometimes beat major cities on price.
howItWorks.tip2: Select several weekends to spot the cheapest departure day in a month.
howItWorks.tip3: Use evening filters if you want to leave after work on Friday.
howItWorks.tip4: Book soon after finding a fare; weekend seats at low prices sell out quickly.
howItWorks.heroImageAlt: Traveler with luggage ready for a weekend trip
howItWorks.weekendImageAlt: European cobblestone street in warm evening light

faq.heroSubtitle: Everything you need to know about finding cheap weekend flights across Europe.
faq.lead: Everything you need to know about finding cheap weekend flights across Europe.

items.q: Can I travel without using vacation days?
items.a: Yes — that is exactly what weekend trips are for. Choose a Fri–Sun pattern and filter for Friday evening departures so you leave after work, enjoy Saturday at your destination, and return Sunday evening. You get a full city break using only your regular weekend.

items.q: Is it safe to book through Kiwi.com?
items.a: Kiwi.com is a major European flight booking platform used by millions of travelers. Payment happens on Kiwi's secure checkout with standard encryption, and you receive a proper booking confirmation with customer support. Many fares also include Kiwi.com booking protection for schedule changes and disruptions — always check the fare conditions before you pay.
A search tool for cheap European weekend flights. We collect round-trip deals for short stays and let you filter by weekend dates, trip length, and departure time.

items.q: Which airports can I search from?
items.a: You can select one or more departure airports across Europe. The site can suggest nearby hubs based on your location and ranks them by deal quality where data is available.

items.q: Can I select multiple weekends at once?
items.a: Yes. Click several date pills in the travel weekend row to combine deals from each weekend. Results grow as you add more weekends.

items.q: What do trip types like Fri–Sun or Fri–Mon mean?
items.a: Trip types filter flights to specific departure days and lengths of stay. For example, Fri–Sun means leaving on Friday and returning on Sunday after two nights at the destination.
Prices are sourced from Kiwi.com flight search data and stored in our database. Final fare, baggage rules, and seat availability are confirmed when you open the booking link.
No. We show deals and link you to the booking partner. You complete purchase on their website.
Our crawler refreshes offers regularly from European departure cities. Very fresh deals may appear within hours; older rows are replaced as new crawls complete.

items.q: Why does a return time sometimes show a dash?
items.a: Return departure times come from detailed route data. If that data is missing for an older offer, we show the return date without a time until the listing is refreshed.
Geolocation is used only in your browser to suggest nearby airports. We do not store your GPS coordinates on our servers. See our privacy policy for details.

faq.heroImageAlt: Travel map and planning notes on a desk

contact.heroSubtitle: Questions, feedback, or partnership ideas? We would love to hear from you.
contact.lead: Questions, feedback, or partnership ideas? We would love to hear from you.
contact.emailBody: We usually reply within a few business days. For booking issues with a specific flight, please contact the airline or travel partner shown on your confirmation.
contact.before1: Check the FAQ for answers about search, trip types, and booking.
contact.before2: Include your departure airport and travel weekend if you are reporting a missing deal.
contact.before3: We cannot change bookings made on Kiwi.com or other partner sites.
contact.heroImageAlt: Laptop and notebook on a desk

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains how euroweekender.com ("we", "us") handles information when you use our website.
privacy.analytics: We use self-hosted Umami Analytics to count page views and basic usage (browser type, country, referrer). Umami does not use cookies and does not profile you across other websites. IP addresses are not stored.
privacy.usage: Standard server logs (IP address, browser type, pages viewed) may be collected by our hosting provider for security and performance.
privacy.location: If you allow browser geolocation, coordinates are used locally to suggest nearby airports. We do not persist GPS data on our servers.
privacy.searchPrefs: Airport and weekend selections are kept in your browser session only and are not linked to a user account.
privacy.thirdParty: Booking links redirect to Kiwi.com and other travel partners. Their privacy policies apply when you leave our site. We use Kiwi.com API data to display flight offers.
privacy.cookies: We do not use advertising cookies. Umami Analytics runs without cookies. Essential technical cookies may be set by our hosting infrastructure. Partner sites may set their own cookies after you click a booking link.
privacy.rights: Depending on your location (including the EU/EEA under GDPR), you may have rights to access, correct, or delete personal data we hold. Contact us to exercise these rights.
privacy.seeAlso: See also our terms of use.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.intro: By using euroweekender.com you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the site.
terms.service: We provide a flight deal search and comparison interface. We are not a travel agency and do not issue tickets. Contracts for transport are between you and the airline or booking partner.
terms.accuracy: Prices, times, and availability come from third-party sources and may change before booking. Always verify details on the partner website before payment. We are not liable for errors in displayed fares or schedules.
terms.affiliate: Some outbound links are affiliate links. We may receive compensation when you book through them. This does not affect the price you pay on the partner site.
terms.acceptable1: Scrape or overload our API or website with automated requests.
terms.acceptable2: Attempt to disrupt service or access non-public systems.
terms.acceptable3: Use the site for unlawful purposes.
terms.liability: The site is provided "as is" without warranties. To the fullest extent permitted by law, we are not liable for indirect or consequential damages arising from use of the site or third-party bookings.
terms.changes: We may update these terms or discontinue features at any time. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.
terms.law: These terms are governed by the laws applicable in the European Union. Any disputes shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the courts where the operator of euroweekender.com is established, without prejudice to mandatory consumer protection rights in your country of residence.
